stories.title: UK and Poland sign new defense and migration pact
stories.summary: The U.K. and Poland signed a defense deal on Wednesday, adding to a growing list of bilateral European security pacts prompted by Russia’s war in Ukraine. Under the Northolt Treaty , the two countries pledged to develop medium-range air defense missiles , deepen cooperation on tackling Russian hybrid threats, bolster joint procurement, ramp up military exercises and restated their commitment to “assist one another” in case of an armed attack. They also agreed to deepen ties on energy and c

stories.title: Brussels watchdog moves to ban Alternative for Germany’s EU party
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The far-right Europe of Sovereign Nations party — home of Alternative for Germany — could be outlawed for failing to uphold EU values. The watchdog that oversees European political parties triggered a process that could result in the ESN being stripped of its right to be a political party and losing its funding. The ESN party is a separate legal entity from the ESN political group in the European Parliament, which is home to 27 MEPs. Political groups are parliamentary factio

stories.title: Von der Leyen to meet Hungarian PM Magyar on Friday
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen will meet Hungary’s newly elected Prime Minister Péter Magyar in Brussels on Friday afternoon, according to the Commission. Maygar said in a social media post on Wednesday evening that the meeting would happen on Friday. The Commission’s chief spokesperson, Paula Pinho, confirmed this and the timing shortly after. The confirmation on Wednesday evening follows several days of apparent hesitation from the EU executive about when —

stories.title: Report ‘phone hack’ to police or I will do it for you, Labour chair tells Farage
stories.summary: Anna Turley gives Reform leader 24 hours to report Russian hacking claim in ‘public and national interest’ The Labour chair has given Nigel Farage 24 hours to report to security services the claim that his phone was hacked by Russia-linked actors or the party will do it for him. In a letter to the Reform UK leader, Anna Turley said it was “in the public and national interest” to ensure that a suspected overseas hack of a senior politician’s phone by a hostile state was properly investigated. Con

stories.title: China accuses Dutch warship of ‘provocative acts’ in South China Sea
stories.summary: Tensions flared Wednesday between China and the Netherlands after a confrontation between a Dutch frigate and the Chinese navy off the disputed Paracel Islands in the South China Sea. Senior Captain Zhai Shichen, spokesperson for the Chinese navy’s Southern Theater Command, said in a statement that naval and air forces used “necessary measures” — including warnings and electronic jamming — after the HNLMS De Ruyter allegedly “illegally intruded” near the Paracel Islands and launched shipbo

stories.title: Norway joins France’s nuclear deterrence club
stories.summary: Norway is joining France’s plans to cooperate more closely with European countries on nuclear deterrence, the two countries’ leaders announced on Wednesday. The addition of Norway, which is traditionally very close to the United States, to the initiative is yet another example of how European countries are rethinking their security strategies in light of Russia’s war on Ukraine and concerns about the Trump administration’s reliability. “Along with nine other Europea

stories.title: Eljárás indult a stockholmi magyar nagykövetség egy munkatársa ellen, mert inzultusba keveredett magyar szomszédjával
stories.summary: Az affért videóra vette a sértett és felesége. A Külügyminisztérium kivizsgálja az ügyet.

stories.title: Germany: Alleged leftist militant found guilty of robbery
stories.summary: Daniela Klette, suspected to be one of the last remaining members of the left-wing terrorist group Red Army Faction, has been found guilty for her part in a series of robberies — but more serious charges await.

stories.title: Woman caught after 30 years on the run is jailed for armed robberies
stories.summary: Klette was finally caught in a flat in Berlin in 2024 and put on trial last year.

stories.title: Verőce polgármestere a Tisza-kormányhoz fordul, hogy mégis világörökségi helyszín legyen az elhíresült verőcei „zsiráfketrec”
stories.summary: Az Orbán-kormány döntése miatt nem lett világörökségi helyszín a verőcei beruházás, de a 300 milliós uniós támogatást más okból kell visszafizetnie a településnek.

stories.title: Ursula von der Leyennel és a NATO főtitkárával is tárgyal a héten Magyar Péter Brüsszelben
stories.summary: A rezidenciáján fogadja Mark Rutte a magyar miniszterelnököt.

stories.title: Utalványt kaptak, akik megígérték, jó helyre szavaznak Kerepesen, a rendőrség meggyanúsított egy lelkészt
stories.summary: Valószínűleg ugyanerről a férfiről forgatott riportot a 444, a lelkész Hankó Balázs exminiszter körzetében tevékenykedett.

stories.title: Európa ott is nagyon izzad, ahol május végén még nem kellene
stories.summary: A kontinens hőkupolával küzd, gyakorlatilag beszorult a meleg. Ennek a következménye, hogy sorra dőlnek a hőségrekordok már május végén: trópusi éjszakák Londonban, tengerbe ugráló és napozó írek, vörös riasztás Rómában, a Szajnához menekülő párizsiak. Így küzd Európa és benne Magyarország a korán érkezett hőséggel, amiből a következő években egyre több lesz.

stories.title: Olyan rendőri vezetőt támad most a Fidesz, akinek 2007-ben még megszavazták a kinevezését
stories.summary: A 2006-ban budapesti főkapitány-helyettesként dolgozó Tóth Gábor lett a Tisza rendészeti államtitkára. A Fidesz most úgy kritizálja őt, hogy korábban arról beszéltek: nem volt felelőssége a 2006-os brutális rendőri fellépésben.

stories.title: 1,1 milliárdért árulja a házát Szijjártó Péter szomszédja, akinek cége milliárdos támogatást kapott a választás előtti napokban
stories.summary: A Herbszt Gábor tulajdonában lévő házban van szivarszoba, gőzkabin és jakuzzi is.

stories.title: Orbán arról, hogy szeretne-e még miniszterelnök lenni: Nem aktuális éppen, úgy látom
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnökkel a Házon Kívül stábja futott össze Felcsúton.

stories.title: Felbontották Fricz Tamás szerződését, nem ír többet a Magyar Nemzetbe
stories.summary: A Békemeneteket szervező CÖF egyik alapítójával levélben közölték a döntést.

stories.title: A százéves árvíz ma már tízévente fordul elő
stories.summary: A közhiedelemmel ellentétben Magyarország nem víznagyhatalom, hanem vízhiányos ország. A Magyarország jövő időben legújabb adásában arról beszélgettünk, mi lehet erre a megoldás.

stories.title: Magyarország lassan drámaivá váló vízhelyzete: mintha 94 százalékban segélyből és kölcsönből élnénk
stories.summary: Elképesztően pazarlók vagyunk a vízzel, miközben hazánk helyzete a legkiszolgáltatottabb az EU-ban. Miért fontos, hogy jobban figyeljünk a vízre, és hogyan tartsunk meg többet? Az Egyensúly Intézet és a Telex közös műsorának legfrissebb epizódja.

stories.title: Helyettes államtitkárként dolgozik majd a három kormányszóvivő
stories.summary: Magyar Péter május 28-i hatállyal nevezte ki Köböl Anitát, Magyar Évát és Szondi Vandát.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely: Meg kell védjem a miniszterelnököt, nem történt heves szóváltás
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a parlament keddi ülését megakasztva lépett oda Gulyás Gergelyhez, miután már nem kapott szót a házelnöktől. Gulyás szerint Magyar csak elmondta, hogy mi lett volna a válasza Gulyás felszólalására.

stories.title: Visszautasította a Fidesz a tiszás politikusok vádaskodását a diplomata-útlevelek ügyében
stories.summary: Azt állítja a korábbi kormánypárt, hogy szélesítette Magyarország nemzetközi kapcsolatrendszerét, ezért kellett sokaknak sokat utazni.

stories.title: Parliament votes to revoke Hungary's withdrawal from ICC
stories.summary: The previous administration initiated the country's withdrawal from the International Criminal Court last spring.

stories.title: The system Orbán built is unraveling before our very eyes
stories.summary: The system Viktor Orbán spent 16 years building has collapsed with unexpected speed. In this video, we show how the most important pillars of NER are collapsing in the wake of Tisza’s landslide victory.

stories.title: Magyar Péter beült közvetlenül Gulyás Gergely elé a parlamentben, így hallgatta a frakcióvezető beszédét
stories.summary: Majd Rétvári Bence beszéde közben visszament a helyére.

stories.title: Ételkiszállítóként is megérkezhet Magyarországra az Uber
stories.summary: Az Uber felvásárlási ajánlatot tett a Foodorát tulajdonló Delivery Heróra. A tulajdonosváltás lehetővé tenné azt is, hogy a Wolt legnagyobb hazai versenytársa az Uber Eats legyen.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor: Jövök
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök Bencsik András véleménycikkére reagált, aki szerint Orbán Viktorra vár a feladat, hogy megszervezze a patrióta mozgalmat.

stories.title: Öt év alatt 100 milliárd forint osztalékot fizetett Orbán Viktor kötélbarátjának cége
stories.summary: Rekordnyereséget ért el tavaly a Market Zrt. A tulajdonosok a profit nagyobb részét a cégben hagyják, de 15 milliárdot azért így is kivesznek.

stories.title: Hont András szerint ne tőle kérdezzék, miért őt találták meg a tehetős, Fidesszel szimpatizáló üzletemberek a félmilliárd forintos támogatással
stories.summary: Szerinte az Öt támogatóinak tetszett, hogy háborúban áll a mainstream médiával, és hogy vele gonoszkodik Magyar Péter.

stories.title: US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García
stories.summary: Judge said Trump administration would not have prosecuted Ábrego had he not challenged his high-profile deportation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive fo

stories.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
stories.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is happening this weekend, despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

stories.title: Israeli military tells residents of swathe of southern Lebanon to leave
stories.summary: Notice to evacuate ‘combat zone’ and head north comes day after Israel launched more than 120 airstrikes on country Israel’s military has told residents across a swathe of southern Lebanon to leave and head north, as the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, said his forces were escalating their offensive against Hezbollah. The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) said in a post on social media that all areas south of the Zahrani River, which runs about 40 kilometres (25 miles) north of the de fact

stories.title: Lifting of internet restrictions reveals Iranians’ anger over food inflation
stories.summary: As government begins restoring connection, population voices dismay over food price increases and shortages The partial lifting of internet restrictions in Iran has revealed a rising tide of anger about food price inflation as ordinary Iranians decry annual price increases of 308% for vegetable oil, 190% for chicken, and 170% for rice. Iranian authorities on Tuesday began restoring the connection to the global internet that was severed on the first day of the US-Israeli war against the Islamic R

stories.title: Revealed: Mandelson vetting warned of ties to senior figures in China, Russia and Israel
stories.summary: Exclusive: Vetting officials also flagged £1m loan when recommending he should be denied security clearance Key questions: did ‘mitigations’ cover links to China and Russia? Peter Mandelson’s associations with senior figures in China, Russia and Israel were among the concerns raised by the UK’s vetting agency when it concluded he should be denied clearance, multiple sources have told the Guardian. Mandelson’s links to China’s minister of finance, Lan Fo’an, the sanctions-hit Russian oligarch Ole

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Eliminate Putin’s ‘last major advantage,’ Zelenskyy urges Washington
stories.summary: Patriots would neutralise ballistic missiles and force Russia to negotiate, says Ukrainian president; half a million Russian dead – GCHQ boss. What we know on day 1,555 Continue reading...

stories.title: US justice department reportedly opens criminal inquiry into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll
stories.summary: Officials said to be examining whether Carroll committed perjury in 2022 deposition tied to lawsuits against president The Trump administration has opened a criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll, the writer who accused the president of sexual assault, according to news reports . Prosecutors, the New York Times and CNN reported on Wednesday, are looking into whether Carroll, 82, committed perjury in a 2022 deposition during her civil lawsuits against Trump, in which she said she did not acce

stories.title: Australia politics live: government sues manufacturer 3M over defence base Pfas contamination in ‘largest ever’ $2bn legal claim
stories.summary: Attorney general says government seeking $2bn in damages to recover costs relating to firefighting foam at 28 defence bases. stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. US justice department reportedly opens criminal inquiry into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll Sign up to the Breaking News US email President Trump is set to hold the 12th cabinet meeting of his second term at 11am EST on Wednesday. Three Democratic state attorneys general said their deputies were turned away from a roundtable hosted by JD Vance on Tuesday, sowing confusion about what the White House has billed as a bipartisan crackdown on fraud. Continue reading...

stories.title: Human rights lawyer Francesca Albanese on life under US sanctions - podcast
stories.summary: The UN’s special rapporteur for Palestine, Francesca Albanese, on the war in Gaza, living under US sanctions and accusations of antisemitism For a brief period this month, the human rights lawyer Francesca Albanese seemed to have some relief. In an extraordinary move in July last year, the Trump administration placed sanctions on the UN’s special rapporteur for Palestine – placing her alongside figures such as Vladimir Putin and Bashir al-Assad – over her engagement with the international crimin

stories.title: Iran war: US 'defensive' strikes target drones, port city command center
stories.summary: The US military said it shot down four Iranian drones and struck a control center in the port city of Bandar Abbas in "defensive" strikes "intended to maintain the ceasefire." DW has more.

stories.title: US carries out new strikes on Iran military site
stories.summary: The US military said it targeted a military site in Bandar Abbas - a strategic port city - and shot down four Iranian attack drones.

stories.title: New NDIS eligibility rules will cut 241,000 participants from scheme in four years, documents reveal
stories.summary: Modelling predicts 241,000 people on the scheme before January 2028 won’t be receiving supports by mid-2031 Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast More than 240,000 participants are expected to be shifted off the national disability insurance scheme in the four years after new eligibility rules are introduced, internal documents reveal. Newly released departmental modelling also shows proposed cuts to funding for social

stories.title: Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers
stories.summary: Mark Carney announces purchase of Saab’s GlobalEye early warning aircraft to patrol Arctic territory Canada has announced plans to buy a fleet of early warning planes from Sweden’s Saab rather than a competing option from Boeing, as the country seeks to reduce reliance on US defense firms. Mark Carney, the prime minister, said on Wednesday that Canada would opt for Saab’s GlobalEye, which is based on Bombardier’s Global 6500 jet. Boeing’s E-7 Wedgetail plane – which has suffered from delays and

stories.title: Brexiters peddled ‘nationalistic pish’, said Reform UK’s Makerfield candidate
stories.summary: Robert Kenyon castigated Brexit as an economically self-harming project on rugby league forum in 2016 Reform UK’s candidate for the Makerfield byelection has castigated Brexit as an economically self-harming project promoted by politicians who “peddled the nationalistic pish”, raising more doubts about his commitment to Nigel Farage’s signature achievement. The comments by Robert Kenyon, unearthed on a defunct rugby league forum and first reported by the Telegraph, follow the emergence of anothe

stories.title: Sexual assault by ex-DUP leader Jeffrey Donaldson left girl ‘feeling sick’, trial hears
stories.summary: Former MP charged with 18 sexual offences involving two alleged victims, and his wife charged with aiding and abetting Jeffrey Donaldson sexually assaulted a child and apologised to her years later at a church-brokered meeting, a court has heard. The former MP and Democratic Unionist party leader allegedly committed the abuse with complicity from his wife, Eleanor Donaldson, a prosecutor told Newry crown court, in County Down, on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. For the latest on Trump’s Iran comments from his cabinet meeting, read our full report: Trump accuses Iran of stalling peace deal to ‘out-wait’ him until US midterms Donald Trump will host the 12th cabinet meeting of his second term on Wednesday as talks on ending the nearly three-month war with Iran reach a crucial stage amid conflicting signals over whether an agreement is close. The gathering had originally been scheduled to take place in the bucolic setting of C

stories.title: Number of Neets in UK could hit 1.25m by early 2030s, Milburn review will say
stories.summary: Urgent action needed to avoid ‘lost generation’, says the former Labour health secretary’s report, due on Thursday Britain risks a 25% rise in the number of young people not in work or education to 1.25 million by the early 2030s without urgent government action to avoid a “lost generation”, a landmark report has warned. Alan Milburn, the leader of the review into why so many young people are economically inactive , said the UK risked opening up a “generational fault line” between young and old

stories.title: Newsom to impose 100% tax on California payees of Trump’s $1.8bn fund
stories.summary: State’s governor looks to thwart US president’s plan to divert money to allies, including January 6 rioters California governor Gavin Newsom is looking to thwart Donald Trump ’s $1.776bn “anti-weaponization fund ” by imposing a 100% tax on any payout received by state residents. In May, the Department of Justice (DoJ) announced a fund to compensate alleged “victims of lawfare and weaponization”. It’s unclear who qualifies under this category. Continue reading...

stories.title: Turkey: Leadership dispute engulfs opposition CHP
stories.summary: The top figures in Turkey's main opposition party differ on how to resolve the leadership vacuum after a court annulled its 2023 primary. One wants the party delegates to pick a leader, the other wants members to vote.

stories.title: Philippines: New inquiry launched into Duterte-era drug war
stories.summary: A former ICC judge and a Catholic bishop will lead the new EJK Truth Commission looking into the bloody anti-drug campaign under Rodrigo Duterte. The former Philippines' president is set to face trial in November.

stories.title: South Africa: 300 Ghanaians leave amid xenophobic tensions
stories.summary: Hundreds of Ghanaians are leaving South Africa on special repatriation flights as anti-immigration protests and violence against foreigners escalate in the richer African nation.

stories.title: Canada to buy Swedish surveillance plane over US models
stories.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney said the Canadian Armed Forces need the plane to detect and deter threats across the Arctic.

stories.title: First Ghanaians evacuated from South Africa over immigration protests land in Accra
stories.summary: It follows recent protests that have led to fears there could be a resurgence in xenophobic violence.

stories.title: Israel issues evacuation order for swathes of southern Lebanon
stories.summary: The military says areas south of the Zahrani River are now "combat zones" as it threatens Hezbollah with fresh strikes.

stories.title: Trump warns Iran to make a deal or 'we'll finish the job'
stories.summary: Speaking at a White House cabinet meeting on Wednesday, the US president said he was unsatisfied with Tehran's offers and in no rush, even as the White House dismissed an Iranian state television report of a draft agreement as a "complete fabrication".

stories.title: New head of Hamas' military wing killed in strike on Gaza, Israel says
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office described Odeh as one of the "architects" of the October 7 attacks.

stories.title: Thousands in Georgia rally for EU membership as country marks 35th independence anniversary
stories.summary: Waving Georgian and EU flags, thousands of protesters marched through central Tbilisi before staging a rally outside parliament amid a heavy police presence.

stories.title: Trump threatens Oman in latest play to open the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: President Donald Trump rejected a plan that would see Oman and Iran jointly charge a toll for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, threatening harsh consequences for the U.S. ally if it follows through on  discussions that have reportedly  taken place with Tehran. “Oman will behave just like everybody else or we’ll have to blow them up,” he told reporters Wednesday at a White House Cabinet meeting. “They understand that. They’ll be fine.” Opening up the strait, a critical transi

stories.title: Turkish police fire water cannon at protesters after opposition leader ousted – video
stories.summary: Riot police in Turkey have fired a water cannon and teargas to break up a rally called by the ousted opposition leader Özgür Özel days after a court dismissed him from office. Özel called the lunchtime rally in İzmir as Turkey was poised to shut down for the four-day Eid al-Fitr holiday, which begins on Wednesday. On Sunday, riot police had battered their way into the main opposition CHP’s headquarters in the capital, Ankara, firing teargas and beating party members before throwing them out, Öze

stories.title: Extreme heat in Europe ‘a brutal reminder’ of climate crisis, UN chief says
stories.summary: Simon Stiell said burning fossil fuels was driving intense heatwaves as UK and France broke temperature records on consecutive days The UN climate chief has said an extreme early heat event sweeping parts of western Europe was “a brutal reminder of the spiralling impacts of the climate crisis”, after France and the UK set new temperature records for May on two consecutive days. Simon Stiell, the executive secretary of the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change, said on Wednesday the “main cul

stories.title: Zelenskyy asks Trump for help with air defences as Russia continues attacks – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed In recent days across parts of Europe, temperatures have soared, heat records have been broken and spring has felt more like the height of summer. Météo France, the French national weather service, has attributed this to a “heat dome”, with warmth held in place by a high-pressure weather front that has produced temperatures more than 10C above what used to be usual for this time of year. Continue reading...

stories.title: WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warns of ‘catastrophic collision of disease and conflict’, as Uganda closes border with DRC The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and confl

stories.title: UK will get no special treatment from EU, European ministers say
stories.summary: There will be ‘no cherrypicking’ of policies, EU says, after Starmer says he hopes to negotiate single market for goods The UK will get no special treatment in its future economic relationship with the EU, European ministers have said, in a further blow to Keir Starmer’s hopes of negotiating a single market for goods. stories.title: ‘Put an end to this war’: Russian director Andrey Zvyagintsev makes new plea to Putin
stories.summary: After winning the Grand Prix at Cannes film festival, the exiled auteur sent a direct message to the Russian president urging him to stop the war Accoladed director Andrey Zvyagintsev has sent a direct message to Vladimir Putin urging him to start listening to the Russian people and end the “senseless” war in Ukraine, continuing a war of words between Russia’s most revered living film-maker and the Kremlin that started at the Cannes film festival awards ceremony over the weekend. “Except for the

stories.title: Nearly half a million Russians killed in Ukraine war, UK spy chief says
stories.summary: Anne Keast-Butler says Russian forces are ‘going backwards on the battlefield’ for first time since late 2022 Nearly half a million Russian soldiers have been killed in Ukraine since the start of Vladimir Putin’s invasion more than four years ago, according to a new estimate from the head of the British spy agency GCHQ. stories.title: Why is Ferrari facing such a backlash to its first electric car?
stories.summary: The Italian marque has broken with the past with its four-door, €550,000 Luce and traditionalists are furious Ferrari is different from other carmakers, and so are its product launches. So revered is the company in its native Italy that among the first people to sit behind the wheel of its first electric vehicle were the country’s president and the pope. Yet judging by the backlash from investors, some critics and – inevitably – a horde of online commenters, the company may need help from a high

stories.title: US building Ebola quarantine center in Kenya for Americans amid outbreak
stories.summary: Some experts criticize White House approach and say not allowing Americans to return to US hurts treatment efforts The Trump administration is building a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya for Americans affected by the Ebola outbreak, instead of bringing them home. The White House on Wednesday confirmed that the US was setting up a facility in Kenya for Americans to quarantine after Ebola exposure in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). Continue reading...

stories.title: Resident doctors in England to stage four-day strike in June
stories.summary: BMA blames new health secretary for decision to stage 16th strike in long-running jobs and pay dispute Resident doctors in England will next month stage the 16th strike in their long-running jobs and pay dispute, and have blamed the new health secretary for their decision. They will strike for four days from 7am on Monday 15 June until 6.59am on Friday 19 June. Announcing the move, the British Medical Association warned that resident doctors would mount a further stoppage in July unless progress

stories.title: Jill Biden says she thought Joe Biden was having a stroke during 2024 debate
stories.summary: In CBS interview set to air Sunday, former first lady says she was ‘frightened’ during husband’s debate with Trump Jill Biden said she had been “frightened” as she watched Joe Biden ’s faltering performance during his 2024 presidential debate, and thought her husband might have suffered a stroke. “I was frightened, because I had never, ever seen Joe like that before or since. Never,” the former first lady said in a 30-second clip of an interview with CBS, set to air on Sunday. Continue reading..

stories.title: Anti-abortion activist concedes pictures of human foetuses may have been sugar glider joeys

In a video posted to Facebook on Wednesday afternoon, Howe said it appeared she

stories.title: Reuters executive Simon Robinson expected to replace Justin Stevens as ABC news director
stories.summary: Exclusive: Robinson, who has been at Reuters for 16 years, will be responsible for about 2,000 staff across the ABC’s news division A top news executive from Reuters, Simon Robinson, is expected to be appointed as news director of the ABC in coming days, Guardian Australia can reveal. He will be the external replacement for Justin Stevens who resigned on Wednesday after four years in the role, citing personal and professional reasons. Continue reading...

stories.title: How the plastic bottle cap became a parable for the value of EU regulation | Alberto Alemanno
stories.summary: Supporters of deregulation want Europe to be more like the US. But that would serve only American interests In July 2024, a European Union law came into force requiring plastic bottle caps to remain attached to their bottles. The regulation was widely mocked by social-media jokesters and Silicon Valley billionaires alike. This, people said, was Brussels at its worst: bureaucrats micromanaging, treating citizens like children who couldn’t be trusted to recycle a cap. What went almost entirely unr

stories.title: Samsung memory chip staff in line for £310,000 bonuses after AI profit-sharing deal
stories.summary: Agreement averts strike and shows latest impact of AI boom as two more chipmakers join $1tn club Employees at Samsung Electronics’ memory chip division are to receive bonuses averaging about £310,000 each through a landmark profit-sharing agreement, as the AI boom drives up chipmakers’ profits. Fears of a strike at Samsung were averted on Wednesday after two unions for the world’s largest memory chipmaker said 74% of the 62,616 workers who cast their votes had backed the deal. Continue reading..

stories.title: India news: Modi warns of heat wave dangers
stories.summary: Heatstroke has killed at least 16 people in southern Telangana state, and Prime Minister Modi has said Indians must heed warning signs. The heat wave has also sparked forest fires in the north.

stories.title: India's drug lifeline to Africa disrupted by Iran war
stories.summary: The fragile logistics corridor via Gulf states that connects African patients with critical Indian pharmaceuticals has been thrown into disarray with the Strait of Hormuz closed.

stories.title: EU enlargement: Is the bloc ready to welcome new members?
stories.summary: Brussels says the EU must grow, but resistance within the bloc remains strong, with some arguing that it must reform before enlarging. Montenegro remains the only country with a realistic chance of joining soon.

stories.title: Germany: No recovery in sight for the economy
stories.summary: The war in Iran has dashed hopes for economic growth. Germany's pension and healthcare systems are also feeling the strain.

stories.title: Ebola: Uganda closes border with DR Congo as virus spreads
stories.summary: Uganda has shut its border "with immediate effect," despite warnings by the WHO that the move could backfire and cause the disease to spread.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump says US will 'watch over' Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said the vital Strait of Hormuz "must be open to everyone" and "no one can control it." He made a threat to Oman, which is negotiating with Iran to control the vital waterway. DW has more.

stories.title: Trump urges Middle East states to sign Abraham Accords
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has urged more Middle Eastern countries to join the Abraham Accords that aim to normalize relations with Israel. What does the agreement entail?

stories.title: FIFA World Cup: New York and New Jersey prosecutors probe 'sky-high' ticket prices
stories.summary: US prosecutors have subpoenaed FIFA over expensive World Cup tickets, saying that the event was "not an invitation to exploit our residents and visitors."

stories.title: Concerns grow over conditions at ICE facility in New Jersey

stories.title: Brazil to revive highway project through Amazon amid concerns
stories.summary: Brazil will invest $75 million to pave a highway that cuts through the center of the Amazon rainforest. Environmentalists argue for strong protections to conserve the ecosystem so crucial to curbing climate change.

stories.title: Spain: Police raid HQ of ruling PSOE Socialist party in graft probe
stories.summary: Police have searched the headquarters of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's PSOE party investigating possible illegal payments. Sanchez meanwhile said he was still standing by former PM Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero.

stories.title: South Africa and Afrikaners reject US claims of humanitarian crisis for white people
stories.summary: Around 6,000 South Africans have moved to the US since the Afrikaner programme started last year, according to the US government.

stories.title: Watch: Moment rescuers find five people trapped in Laos cave
stories.summary: Rescuers in Laos have found five villagers alive inside a flooded cave after they were trapped for a week.

stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said fighting in DR Congo was hampering efforts to stop spread

stories.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for 41 months over actor's ketamine death
stories.summary: Kenneth Iwamasa, who pleaded guilty last year to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine causing death, said he "will forever regret" his actions.

stories.title: The world's carmakers are struggling to compete with China

stories.title: How my brother went from liberal Hollywood actor to manosphere 'messiah'
stories.summary: The sister of Mexican influencer El Temach tells the BBC she does not recognise him any more.

stories.title: Is 'out of control' US tipping culture spreading overseas?

stories.summary: Ken Paxton's victory sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November's midterm elections.

At the end of last year, the Guardian revealed that the former Miteni plant in Vicenza had been acquired by the Indian company Laxmi Organic Industr

stories.title: Middle East crisis: Iran’s foreign ministry says US broke ceasefire with overnight strikes – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. For the latest, read our full report: Iran remains in peace talks despite first US strikes since ceasefire Iranian supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei said on his Telegram channel that Gulf powers will no longer be a shield for US bases and the US will no longer have a safe haven in the region, as Tehran and Washington discuss a framework to end their three-month-old war, Reuters reports. The post follows overnight attacks on Iran by the US, testing the ceasefire agreed

stories.title: Putin offers debt relief to new recruits for Ukraine war
stories.summary: New recruits for Russia's war in Ukraine will have debts up to 10 million rubles canceled under a presidential decree. Another new law reportedly foresees the use of Russian armed forces to protect citizens abroad.

stories.title: Israeli strike on village in eastern Lebanon kills 12 as Israel calls up more troops
stories.summary: The Israeli military did not comment on this particular strike, but said on Monday that it was targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in eastern Lebanon.

stories.title: Iran's internet flickers back on despite judicial halt, reports claim
stories.summary: Live metrics show partial restoration of connectivity after President Pezeshkian ordered the international internet to be reopened, but an Iranian court has suspended the order before it could fully take effect, leaving Iranians in legal and digital limbo on day 88 of the country's longest blackout.

stories.title: Dissident detained in South Korea after fleeing China in rubber boat
stories.summary: Dong Guangping has tried to escape on several previous occasions after being jailed for his activism in China A Chinese dissident has washed up on the shores of South Korea after attempting to flee China in a rubber boat. Dong Guangping, 68, is in custody in South Korea, having been detained by the coastguard on Monday evening. He is thought to have travelled more than 30 hours by sea to reach the shores of China’s democratic neighbour. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Mind-bogglingly crazy’: climate experts alarmed by deadly spring heatwaves searing Europe
stories.summary: Scientists warn of ‘new reality’ of heat extremes that claim three times more lives than car crashes and 16 times as many as murderers Malcolm Mistry knew it was going to get “very warm, very quickly” on Monday morning but a slow start out of bed delayed his plans for an early game of cricket with his son. It was already 10am by the time the pair arrived at the sun-soaked nets of their local club in south-west London, and to the embarrassment of the 48-year-old scientist, who played cricket in h

stories.title: Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing
stories.summary: Video appears to show divers discovering group sitting on a rock surrounded by flood water Rescuers have reached five of seven people who have been trapped for a week in a flooded, remote cave in Laos, after days spent navigating narrow, inundated passageways amid persistent rain. Video footage shared on social media by rescue divers showed the five men crouched together on a rocky ledge in a dark cave chamber, surrounded by muddy water. “There’s no need to cry,” one of the rescue team told the

stories.title: Egy titkos kormányhatározatra hivatkozva vettek ki legalább százmilliárd forintnyi kiadást az idei költségvetésből
stories.summary: 176 milliárd forint hiányzik az idei költségvetésből csak az autópálya-koncesszió kiadásai közül. Az új kormányon múlik, nyilvánosságra hozzák-e a hiányt okozó döntés hátterét.

stories.title: For Pakistan, joining Abraham Accords is a tricky choice
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump wants Pakistan and some other Muslim-majority nations to normalize ties with Israel. While joining the Abraham Accords has its benefits for Islamabad, the repercussions would be immense.

stories.title: Germany hopes for heat pump boom amid Iran war
stories.summary: The war in the Middle East is threatening fossil fuel supplies and leading fuel and gas prices to rise in Germany. Could that drive a surge in demand for heat pumps?

stories.title: Europe's energy problem isn't green power — it's storage

stories.summary: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

stories.title: Greenpeace stages record deep-sea protest against seabed mining

stories.title: Saudi Arabia: More than 1.5 million pilgrims perform stoning ritual in Mina
stories.summary: More than 1.5 million Muslim pilgrims gathered in Mina, Saudi Arabia, on 27 May to perform the symbolic stoning ritual that marks a key stage of the annual Hajj pilgrimage.

stories.title: Egypt marks record year for gas discoveries amid domestic shortage
stories.summary: Egypt’s recent Eni-linked gas finds have lifted hopes of an upstream revival, but analysts say import pressure is unlikely to ease soon.

stories.title: Bodies pulled from rubble near Tyre after deadly Israeli strike in Lebanon
stories.summary: Lebanon rescue workers recovered bodies from the rubble in Burj al-Shamali near the coastal city of Tyre on 27 May, one day after an Israeli strike that Lebanon’s health ministry said killed at least 14 people.

stories.title: Ghana welcomes Pope's apology over Catholic Church's role in slavery

stories.title: Trump Board of Peace's official Gaza reconstruction fund is empty, source says

stories.summary: More people regret knee surgery than abortion. So why is the patriarchy still scaring us with lifelong torment? Ireland’s parliament, the Dáil, voted down a reproductive rights amendment bill this month that would have abolished the country’s mandatory three-day waiting period for access to an abortion. stories.title: The wind boom Trump couldn't stop
stories.summary: Even with fierce opposition, offshore wind projects are multiplying along US coasts — signaling a massive shift in energy.

stories.title: Gulf braces for Iran's response as US strikes shake peace talks
stories.summary: Tehran has vowed a "far more severe" response beyond the region after overnight US strikes on missile sites and boats planting mines in southern Iran, even as both sides say negotiations toward a Hormuz deal are still on.

stories.title: North Korea fires projectiles including ballistic missile into Yellow Sea, Seoul says

stories.title: South Korea overpass collapse kills three in Seoul

stories.title: Turkish police use water cannon ahead of deposed opposition leader's speech in Izmir
stories.summary: The CHP is level with the ruling Justice and Development Party (AKP) in most recent opinion polls and although the next election is not due until 2028, many expect Erdoğan to push for early elections.

stories.summary: Az Egyesült Államok extra tesztelést és karantént is belengetett az érintett területekről érkezőknek, az ebola viszont nem olyan, mint a koronavírus, sokkal közelebbi kontaktus kell a fertőzéshez.

stories.title: Az Amazon kinyírta a régi Kindle-öket. Mutatunk néhány alternatívát
stories.summary: Az Amazon május 20-tól nem támogatja a 2013 előtt gyártott könyvolvasóit, amiket ezzel tulajdonképpen mutatós papírnehezékké változtatott. Van azonban megoldás! Arra is, ha teljesen elhagyná az Amazont, és arra is, ha új életet lehelne régi olvasójába.

stories.title: Bad Bunny egy játékpizza hangjaként kapott szerepet a Toy Story ötödik részében
stories.summary: Az ikonikus filmsorozat ötödik része június közepén érkezik a mozikba.

stories.title: A babydoll ruhámat csak a halott kezeimből vehetitek ki
stories.summary: Olivia Rodrigo hálóingszerű ruháját szétszedték az interneten. A kommentelők szerint nem szabad kislányos ruhát hordani, mert az kellemetlen férfitekinteteket vonz. Megnéztük, honnan ered a babydoll, hogyan tapadtak hozzá ezek az asszociációk, és divattervezővel jártunk utána, hogyan kell jól viselni.

stories.title: 800 millió forintos áron cserélhet gazdát egy Artúr király mondáját tartalmazó középkori kézirat
stories.summary: A Szent Grál keresését elmesélő kötet már 700 éve magántulajdonban van, az illusztrációi pedig olyanok, mintha Roy Lichtenstein rajzolt volna a 14. században Batman-képregényeket.

stories.title: Megszüntették az anyjának kamu parlamenti állást intéző halásztelki polgármester bűnügyi felügyeletét
stories.summary: A törvényszék közleménye szerint már nem áll fenn annak veszélye, hogy a polgármestert megnehezítené vagy meghiúsítaná a bizonyítást, ezért elengedték.

stories.title: Berlin Hauptbahnhof at 20: A rail hub with symbolic power
stories.summary: Berlin's central station was inaugurated in May 2006. Beyond its functionality, the modern glass-and-steel structure was built to play a key role in reunified Germany.

stories.title: Unseasonal May heat wave grips Europe
stories.summary: The UK and France have registered record May temperatures as western Europe swelters under a "heat dome." Such extremes are occurring more often as the planet warms.

stories.title: Where are US forces stationed in Europe?
stories.summary: The United States operates various bases across the European continent. DW breaks down what these sites are used for, and how many troops are stationed there.

stories.title: Belgium: Train collides with school bus, several dead
stories.summary: A train has crashed into a school bus near a town close to Brussels, killing at least four people, including two children.

stories.title: Ferrari unveils its first all-electric car, the 4-door Luce
stories.summary: The Italian supercar giant has released images of what will be both its first ever pure EV, and its first car that can seat five people. The Luce eyes a younger clientele and the increasingly competitive Chinese market.

stories.title: Ukraine: EU, Germany summon Russian envoys after Moscow tells diplomats to leave Kyiv
stories.summary: The EU and Germany both summoned Russian diplomats to protest attacks on Kyiv and orders for diplomats to leave the city. The UN secretary-general also rebuked Russia in a session on proliferating conflicts worldwide.

stories.title: How did Berlin's Jewish Hospital and hundreds of its patients survive the Holocaust?
stories.summary: When Red Army troops entered Berlin's Jewish Hospital in 1945, they were astonished to discover hundreds of Jews living just a couple of miles away from Hitler's bunker.

stories.title: Western Europe swelters as late May heat dome shatters records
stories.summary: Western Europe faces record late-May heat, with France, the UK and Spain sweltering up to 11C above normal and officials warning of rising health risks.

stories.title: Gaza’s displaced brace for a bleak Eid al‑Adha amid war and soaring prices
stories.summary: Displaced families in Gaza Strip are facing another difficult Eid al-Adha, as war, shortages and soaring prices leave many unable to buy sacrificial animals or properly observe one of Islam’s most important religious celebrations.

stories.title: More than 500,000 students enter Tashkent maths Olympiad as STEM interest grows
stories.summary: The TasIMO competition brought together 350 finalists from Europe and Asia following a sharp rise in entries.

stories.title: Search operation continues for seven villagers trapped in a flooded Laos cave
stories.summary: There has been no official confirmation on why the villagers went into the cave, although the rescuers involved said that they went in to look for gold deposits.

stories.title: Dozens of drones fall from sky during Sydney festival show
stories.summary: A major drone show at Sydney’s Vivid festival was cancelled after nearly 90 drones malfunctioned and fell into Darling Harbour during a live performance.

stories.title: Watch: Bridge collapses and car swept away in China river
stories.summary: The driver and passengers of the car managed to escape before the bridge gave way in Xiaogan.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia: Hajj 2026 reaches its spiritual peak as millions of Muslim pilgrims gather
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia entered the most significant stage of the 2026 hajj on 26 May as millions of pilgrims dressed in white gathered at Mount Arafat near Mecca and circled the Kaaba inside the Grand Mosque.

stories.title: Record May heat puts France’s climate preparations to the test as heat dome swelters Europe
stories.summary: France has quite literally been acting out the best way to cope during extreme temperatures. Now, it’s trapped under a heat dome.

stories.title: First footage released after deadly train and school minibus crash in Belgium
stories.summary: Belgium emergency services were deployed on 26 May after a train collided with a school minibus at a level crossing in Buggenhout, in East Flanders, killing several people and seriously injuring others.

stories.title: US and Armenia pledge to move forward on economic corridor during Rubio visit
stories.summary: Marco Rubio visited Yerevan, backing PM Pashinyan’s pro‑western course and signed TRIPP corridor and strategic partnership deals to boost regional peace.

stories.title: Iran condemns US strikes as 'gross violation' of ceasefire
stories.summary: The attacks took place while Iranian and Qatari negotiators were in Doha for peace talks.

stories.title: Paris 'punishingly hot' as Western Europe hit by heatwave
stories.summary: Hugh Schofield reports from Paris as a heat dome settled over Western Europe bringing temperatures above the average for May.

stories.title: Nasa unveils next steps to build permanent Moon base
stories.summary: Nasa plans to send hopping drones and roving vehicles to the Moon as part of plans for a permanent Moon base.

stories.title: Watch: UFC arena construction begins at White House ahead of fight
stories.summary: The massive structure will host a cage fight as part of celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: ‘She does not back down’: the couple seeking to legalise same-sex marriage in Botswana
stories.summary: Bonolo Selelo and Tsholofelo Kumile are going to court for right to wed but face fierce opposition from church groups Bonolo Selelo was at Botswana’s national museum for a Gaborone Pride event when she spotted Tsholofelo Kumile and was struck by her good looks. The two initiated a conversation and when Kumile expressed anxiety about what a tarot reading at the event might hold, Selelo thought nothing of offering her a hug. The reading turned out positive but Kumile claimed her hug anyway and the

stories.title: Why are Europe's biggest defense projects in trouble?
stories.summary: Disputes over leadership, military priorities and industrial control are threatening to fracture Europe's €100 billion fighter jet project — with tensions now spilling into a Franco-German tank project as well.

stories.title: Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse
stories.summary: Inmates at Barinas prison allege they were peacefully protesting when prison staff opened fire, leaving some wounded Inmates at Venezuela’s western Barinas prison staged a protest on its roof on Sunday, piling flaming mattresses and calling for the removal of the facility’s director, whom they accused of overseeing guards as they shot unarmed prisoners. “We want justice. They are shooting us, the guards and the wardens,” a prisoner said in a video shared by the Venezuelan Observatory of Prisons,

stories.title: Weather tracker: flash floods in New York and a heat dome in Europe
stories.summary: Rain overwhelms sewer system in parts of US city, while temperatures in France break May record New York City saw flash flooding on Wednesday, as large parts of Brooklyn and Queens received about 2in (50mm) of rainfall in as little as 20 minutes. Officials said the deluge caused water to flow into the sewer system at a rate of up to 6in an hour, quickly overwhelming an aged network that was designed to accommodate just 1.75in an hour. Residents and commuters found themselves wading knee-deep thr

stories.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
stories.summary: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate action The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediat

stories.title: Blinded by the light pollution: Cities seek to restore night
stories.summary: Artificial lights are blotting out the moon and stars, harming human health, and disrupting the natural world. Why is this a problem? And how can we stop it without sitting in the dark?
